source_lists.title: Wuxia
source_lists.desc:
I was planning on making my own Wuxia list, then I found End of Cinema's 30 Essential Wuxia Films, a great starting list for those looking for Wuxia films.

The first 32 films comprise the End of Cinema list, 33 and on are additional films I may add.

source_lists.title: Wuxia
source_lists.desc:
Wuxia (wu-hsia), which literally means "martial hero", is a broad genre of Chinese fiction concerning the adventures of martial artists. Although wuxia is traditionally a form of literature, its popularity has caused it to spread to diverse art forms such as Chinese opera, manhua, films, television series and video games. It is a component of popular culture in many Chinese-speaking communities around the world.

The word "wuxia" is a compound word composed of the words wu (lit. "martial", "military", or "armed") and xia (lit. "honourable", "chivalrous", or "hero"). A martial artist who follows the code of xia is often referred to as a xiake (lit. "follower of xia") or youxia (lit. "wandering xia"). In some translated works of wuxia, the… more

source_lists.title: Wuxia
source_lists.desc: Um subgênero do cinema de artes marciais chinês. Os filmes de Wuxia, ou filmes de esgrima, são histórias de aventura ambientadas na China antiga, geralmente com elementos de fantasia e misticismo. Comparados com os filmes de kung fu mais realistas, os filmes Wuxia são mais estilizados, com tramas elaboradas, cenários luxuosos e cenas de luta cuidadosamente coreografadas e que desafiam a gravidade, feitas com o auxílio de fios.
